review_text:
Awesome job Kellie and the team. The heart of Hawker is now Mt Little Station.
Great facilities, fantastic pizzas but most importantly, a glimpse into Australian outback culture and values I've found nowhere else.

This place is SPECIAL, views of the Flinders Ranges surround you, there is not a bad campsite, each one has incredible beautiful views and they are all super private….not to mention flushing toilets scattered around the station.
Parts of the famous Heysen and Mawson trails cross the property so the walks here are stunning, no need to leave
An absolutely fantastic open air tavern in the middle of the station thqt has pizza nights twice a week, complete with a wood burning pizza oven and a really well made dough, and a great selection of local beers and mixed drinks
We spent 5 nights in the homestead, 4 double bedrooms two bathrooms and three toilets, a great kitchen and THE BEST fire pit overlooking the ranges with firewood replenished whenever you need
We’re already planning our return
It’s central to Hawker and so many other walks
Can’t recommend Mount Little enough
Such a wonderful place to stay and explore! Perfect if you have kids, the staff are lovely and very helpful! If you’re lucky enough to be there on pizza night it’s a must they are yummy!
We have been a few times to Mt Little. We can’t fault it! Fantastic place for families and groups with dogs.

Stayed here twice now in the past year and loved every minute of it, sites are very large and facilities great for Station Stay Bush camping. Tavern is always a hit with great food and company. Will certainly be back again
Great place to get away and feel the breeze. Secluded campsites, some good walking and 4wd tracks, wildlife.

Thank you Kellie for a great stay on your station! We had a marvellous site for our 2 vans right on the creek bed. The view from the top of the 4WD track was stunning. Fantastic amenities. The springs in Mayo Gorge were beautiful. The little store was so unique. Highly recommend.

We stayed one of the Sunset Cabins over the Easter weekend and wow, what a spot!
The view, the room, the atmosphere, the food (omg the pizza yummm), so much to do with the kids (I was solo with Mr 11 and Miss 7) and not hard on the vehicle!
Swimming in rock pools such an amazing place
Kellie, Peter and the whole team were truly amazing! Such a relaxed few days exploring such an amazing part of SA!

We absolutely love our time at Mount Little Station. The property is beautiful, everything is well planned out and Peter and Kellie are very accommodating. They've clearly put a lot of time and effort into their property, establishing 4WD tracks and beautiful hikes. Our son also loved the animals and we all had a great time at pizza night. Highly recommend.

Spent Easter Sunday at Mt Little with some friends camping, couldn’t rate it high enough. The owner is awesome, lovely staff getting around it it and great vibe for pizza / live music. So many 4WD tracks to go off yourself. Most  importantly, the toilet / shower facilities were spotless both days. Thanks for an awesome stay

I had such a great couple of nights stay at the Mt Little Station! Kellie was very welcoming and informative upon my arrival. I lucked out with being early in the season and having most of the place to myself with so much to see and explore on the property. All the services and amenities were so well presented and maintained, plenty of camping space and well maintained camp area.
I highly recommend staying at this Station stay if in the Flinders area!

We stayed at Mount little station in our caravan with two small children. It had been recommended to us by a couple of other young families and it did not disappoint. We had an amazing 3 nights. Our campsite was private and the most beautiful campsite we have visited in 7 months on the road. The facilities were spotless too. Our days were filled with adventures around the huge property.

Our highlights were:
Animal feeding with Kelly. Although it was the end of the tourist season she made us feel like we were important guests and we loved our mornings helping her feed the animals.

Finding the camels on a drive and feeding them from our car, it felt like we were on a safari and our children loved it (me too)
They were so friendly and gentle.

- Finding the treasure in the pirate cave, such a fun hike to keep our 4 year old amused and we were so excited to get to go inside the cave at the end. A great family adventure.

- Walking mayo gorge (perfect distance with littles)

- Animal maze. Our daughter loved figuring out with way to go and guess the animals.

- Pizza night! Absolutely best pizza ever! Beautiful tavern and family friendly.

Highly recommend this stay to families. We are sad we left as we could have easily filled a whole week here.

Stayed here for a night on our way back to Vic from Coober Pedy. Absolutely loved it, having spent time on working farms and stations before, we had an idea what to expect, but this exceeded our expectations. Kellie was very friendly and helpful explaining the do's and don'ts. We were amazed on how much free run we got on the property, considering its a working farm. The campsites are massive, the station map is not to scale, but very easy to read and follow. Yes there are flies, its the Outback. We can highly recommend staying here. We have read some of the previous reviews and we would advise ignoring the low reviews as it seems some people don't understand the term working station or Outback. Keep up the great hospitality Kellie, we loved it.

We had the most amazing few days, enjoying this little piece of heaven. Kellie was so welcoming and helpful. There was so much to enjoy, the landscape, the history and the animals. I never thought I would get that close to a camel let alone get kisses ❤️ Do yourself a favour and go and visit.

We stayed here for 6 nights, and we had a ball. The hosts were great. The Pizza nights are a treat. The showers are clean and tidy. Plenty of hot water as long as you had the sun out. The camp kitchen is very basic and rough.  We had no need to use it.|The landscape is amazing. If you're a photographer and into landscapes, you won't be disappointed as long as you get off your butt and explore the many areas on this property....I'd stay here again. On advice, we went to Rawnsley Park for a look. Better facilities for sure,  but way too crowded,  just like a normal caravan park with everyone next to each other. The price is on the high side for a non-powered no water site, but the space and peace are worth the extra in my books.

We had the most incredible stay at Mount Little Station - our 4 family groups were given area 14 and it could not have been more perfect! We had an amenities block nearby but far enough away from everyone to feel alone. We spent a whole day just exploring the property and 4WD tracks, there was so much to see! The pizza night was an absolute hoot, the host was very entertaining and pizzas just delicious! Our kids thoroughly enjoyed feeding the animals and driving through the paddock with the donkeys and camels with a dip in the gorge to finish our stay!! We are already planning our next visit, we LOVED IT!! Thanks for having us Peter, Kellie and crew 🙌🏻

We didnt get there rooms we got a non powered camp site and had a blast the wood oven pizza topped it off we loved there old rain water tank turned into a shower n toliet, we are plaining to go again when its cooler too cant wait

Had the best relaxing time camping at Mt Little Station recently.
Sites are a good size, good flat cleared ground with great fire pits.
The firewood you can buy is good burning & very generous with the supply.
Pizza night was great, delicious pizzas and great entertainment from all the staff.
Kellie & Peter are great hosts, making everyone who stays there welcome & special.
I loved your backyard, will definitely spend time there again in the future.

4x4 driving on the Station
Great pizza night with fabulous hosts
Would highly recommended a visit

It was more than your normal station stay.  We loved the qwerky signs and the tank toilet was a gem! We had such a lovely campsite too. So much to do or not do.You can plan your own adventure each day. Fabulous views of the ranges whichever way you looked. Especially on top of Mt Little. Beautiful walks. Our grandies especially loved feeding the animals and the Pirate Cave! And the pizzas are to die for!! What a great job you guys are doing.😀
A fantastic setup, great camping sites along the station road. We had delicious pizzas on Wed night.
Don’t stay in the Dongas over priced no electricity and camp kitchen had no gas for cooking

A group of us (12) stayed at the homestead for a week in August.  What a fabulous time we had! Kellie went above and beyond in organising transport to and from trailheads each day, as well as ensuring we had everything we needed in the homestead. I left a few things behind in my room at the end of the stay and Kellie kindly posted them to me. We all loved our time at Mt Little station. Highly recommend!

We stayed at Mt Little Station for two nights and camped at one of their many unpowered, spacious and scenic camping spots. The station has lots of magnificent self drive or walking track options which can be experienced according to your own capabilities and expectations.
Kelly (owner) provides very welcoming hospitality and makes guests feel most comfortable in their excellent surrounds and accommodation.
Campfires are available at campsites which allows for self wood supply or you can purchase bagged wood at the office.
A clean and tidy solar heated shower/toilet ablution and camp kitchen is also within close proximity to some campsites.
We thoroughly enjoyed this stopover on the Outback Way.

We spent 3 nights here and it was such an amazing stay. The station is enormous, so much to do on the property and the pizza night at “the tavern” on the Saturday was one of the highlights - sitting around the fire sharing stories and places to go. The owners Kellie and Pete are the friendliest people especially on the evening of their big golf day - so many laughs.
We visited the gorges, went to Blinman and to the Praire Hotel where they sell feral food.
Would definitely recommend a stay at Mt Little Station.
The sunsets were magical!

We had an amazing stay at Mt Little Station. There are plenty of spacious campsites to choose from that range from intimate little one van sites through to sites capable of handling large groups of caravans travelling together.
The tracks are clearly marked and easy to drive, providing full access to the entire property. So many historical ruins to explore and a great museum and gift store on the tracks!
As a working cattle farm and sheep station, don’t be surprised to wake finding your campsite being shared cows or sheep grazing on the grass.
The amenities are clean and adequate, and the showers are HOT!
You can BYO firewood or buy on site, as well as the obligatory souvenir ranging from your classic bumper sticker though to hats, t-shirts and jumpers.
We had a fantastic stay. The hosts are very friendly and welcoming.

We stayed at Mount Little Station for 2 nights. What a magical spot it is. The views of the Alder Range every morning was lovely to wake up to. We walked the Mayo Gorge, drove the 4WD track up Mount Little and had pizza’s at the Tavern on Saturday night. The sites are enormous, this station has so much to offer ….its a must stay. We will be back for sure.

Totally fabulous place! Stayed in the homestead for 6 nights to walk parts of the Heysen trail. Kellie could not have been more helpful including driving us to the start of the walks and collecting us. Extremely well equipped kitchen so we mostly cooked but had great pizza on arrival from the tavern next door. Wonderful fire pit with lots of wood and a view to die for, and heaps of stars! Book now!

If you are looking for a quiet, unique place to camp in the Flinders Ranges during the winter months, then look no further than Mt Little Station. Amazing outback views, stunning creek side camping, open fires, friendly and hospitable hosts, pizza nights, blue skies, sunshine and awesome sunsets! What more could you ask for!

Mt Little Station Townhouse in Hawker was a fun place to stay.
With three generous bedrooms, well equipped kitchen, good bathroom & 2nd toilet, open fireplace & comfortable lounge.
We enjoyed the warmth of the fire at night.
Beds are comfy.  Location is great. Would stay here again..

We had a great long weekend at Mount Little Station. The Sunset Apartment was great and had an amazing view over the Elder Range. Great hosts, delicious pizzas and enough to see and do within the station grounds.

our first station stay and it absolutely exceed our expectations!
site 13 was excellent - secluded, immersed in nature but could also walk 500m to the tavern! sites are huge with privacy and camp fire 🔥

on arrival Kellie greeted us and was warm and friendly and given amazing pack with lots of local info, walking tracks and pizza nights.

attended the pizza night on Wednesday - great vibe and huge range of pizza. had local gin and beer that were great.

spent 4 days exploring station with one day trip through brachina gorge.
kids loved feeding animals in the morning, bottle fed lambs and calves. then went to feed camels and donkeys etc.

did the black goat hike (kids age 6 and 8) and the pirate cave hike as well as mayo gorge and rock pools and all were perfect for our kids. lots of fun 4wd tracks.

we will be back!

if i could give Mt Little Station more than 5 stars i would. what a wonderful place. spent 2 nights camping and exploring the property. 27,000 acres of pure magic.
incredible views. excellent facilities (toilets/shower/camp kitchen)

the best welcome pack on arrival with all the information you could need.

Firewood can be purchased at check in.
make sure you visit when they have pizza night at the tavern!

so many things to see and do, visit the pets (camels donkeys goats cows and sheep), mayo gorge, roy's relic museum, the skull tree is a must, settlers ruins scattered throughout the property.

carry cash if you can, they do have card facilities if needed.

This place is awesome, our kids loved the experience of being out on a farm. Camping spots were well placed so you felt like you were on your own. Pizza night at the tavern also a highlight. We stayed for 4 nights, but could have stayed here for longer as didn’t explore the entire area properly. Staff were so helpful with what to do and where to go. Highly recommend this experience.

Mt Little station, didn’t disappoint.
We had a great stay. The weather was wet and windy but didn’t take away from the amazing experience. We stayed in the caravan so can’t comment on the rooms.
Had pizza at the tavern, open Wednesday and Saturday nights. Yum go the meat lovers and Nutella !
Did the 4x4 track and went for a walk to the mayo gorge.
Grab yourself a sticker, stubbie holder and hat pin.
Kellie and the crew have done a super job. Hats off to you guys for creating such a wonderful raw and authentic holiday experience and
Put this place on your list, don’t miss it. 5 stars .

The station was such a cool place to camp. The camp kitchen + showers were surprisingly high quality and being able to see wildlife so close to our campsite was incredible. The 4WD track up Mount Little was incredibly fun to drive as well.
awesome place to explore! pizza night is a must yum! and the animal feeding too, great sites and so much to see 🌵

Our family and friends stayed for 3 nights in July 2025. Our friends bought their dog and we had our three year old son. Our spot was spacious and well kept. We attended the pizza night on Wednesday (amazing), we did the 4WD tracks and did the baby animal feeding every morning. Our three year old loved himself sick the whole time! He particularly loved the pirate map given in the welcome pack, where we went off and searched for the pirate cave. He’s still talking about it.

The owners are relaxed and welcoming. Their staff were lovely to deal with. Always up for a chat, super helpful! We even got to do an extra baby animal feeding as we turned up to the homestead at just the right time. Our son had a particular affinity with a tiny baby goat.

We had a lot of wildlife at the camp too. Sheep, alpaca, cow, parrots, owls, possums and goats.
Showers and toilets available around camp.
Firewood was available when we were there.
Some people have commented to us that the price is “steep” but we feel that we’d pay even more for the experience we had. 10/10.

We’ve been twice now and we can’t wait to come back. Wish we could have stayed longer.

Such a great experience to stay here.  Wonderful hosts with so many walks and 4wd tracks to experience as well as all the animals on the property.

Twice a week they have Pizza Night up at the tavern which is a must to experience.
We really enjoyed our short stay, great camping locations and ammenities with picturesque hiking and trails on the station. We totally agree with many other 5 star review recommendations.

We had a wonderful 4-night stay at Mt Little Station in June 2025.
Kellie was prompt and clear in her email communication during the booking process. She outlined our options as city boys who wanted our creature comforts and were travelling with a dog and in a Ford Mustang (rather than the standard 4WD/campervan in this area).
We stayed in one of the Sunset Apartments. It was extremely comfortable, with an electric blanket, air con, strong hot shower, bar fridge, toaster, kettle and Nespresso machine).
The spacious deck provided stunning views out to the Elder Range and up to the brilliant night sky. There were kangaroos hopping and emus strolling by. Our on-leash dog was beside herself.
The 2 Irish backpacker roustabouts were charming and helpful. The pizza at the Tavern was excellent.
We are looking forward to our next visit, which will be with friends visiting from the UK. It will be a uniquely Australian experience for them.

Mount Little is a great place to stay, great easy to find camp grounds, good tracks for day trips with plenty to see and do, Host where amazing people with help and advice given anytime. Pizza night under the stars was amazing. good food and great people to talk too. We will being going back with friends and family for another trip.

Mt Little Station is a gem amongst the spectacular scenery of the Elder Range in the Flinders Ranges.||We stayed a few days & nights and loved every minute.||The owners are lovely and super committed to making their guests feel welcome and inclusive. The helpers were great, too. Always a smile on their faces and ready for a yarn.||The pizzas, were just divine. We have travelled far and wide and rarely ate a pizza that tasty and good.||The animals everywhere were another highlight which all members of our group enjoyed. Sheep, alpacas, donkeys, roos, birds galore...||And on it goes. ||The tracks are best suited to 4-wheel drives as some are stony and rocky. But when one travels in the outback away from towns, if one is smart enough one knows that. It's just common sense... 😊||We did use the camping showers which were incredibly modern. We were told that they' d been renovated over last summer. And yes, they operate on solar. So again, common sense tells one that solar doesn't work at night. No sun-no hot water. ||I'm honestly amazed what the owners and staff have created here for the city plebs like us.||We will certainly go back there and highly recommend Mt Little Station to any traveller who doesn't get their panties in a twist when their warbled expectations are not met. 😘||To be granted access to a working sheep station as a tourist is a privilege we should never take for granted and always respect.

We had a fabulous 4-night stay at Mt Little Station in June 2025. |Kellie was prompt and clear in her email communication during the booking process, outlining our best options as city boys who like our creature comforts, with a dog, and in a Ford Mustang rather than the standard 4WD for the area. |We stayed in one of the Sunset Apartments. Extremely comfortable (nice bed, electric blanket, air con, good hot shower, bar fridge, microwave, Nespresso machine etc). The view from the deck out to the Elder Range and up at night to the brilliant stars was spectacular. It was great to see kangaroos hopping and emus strolling past. |The 2 Irish backpacker roustabouts were charming and helpful. The pizza at the Tavern was delicious. |We can’t wait to return. Next time it will be with friends visiting from the UK. We want to give them a uniquely Australian experience. |Neil, Simon and Hamburger (the dog)

After South Aussie with Cosi gave this station such a great review we were extremely disappointed with our experience. Campsites are nice and large and the views are great. The large animals are great for the kids to see but that’s where it stops. The tracks exploring the property are not accessible by non-4x4 vehicles as you are lead to believe when checking in. You need to have the clearance to navigate the rocks and the creeks. Track maintenance has not been carried out for quite some time. The camp kitchen it’s terrible. Both bbqs are not in safe working order. There is no hot water to the kitchen for dishes etc and the lighting…. What lighting! When the weather is bad don’t think you can seek refuge in the kitchen. Shelter is insufficient. Told to shower during the day as there will not be hot water otherwise. And if you happen to mention your unsatisfactory experiences to staff don't expect any reasonable customer service. Expect to be made to feel an inconvenience, degraded and ill equipped for camping. After camping for 20+ years and staying both in caravan parks and off grid I’m pretty sure I’m equipped and educated enough to make a judgement. For the price I would not stay here and I would not recommend.

Fabulous stay at Mt Little Station. Our accommodation was spectacular and the hospitality from our hosts Kellie and Pete was amazing. Location is super convenient and so much to do and see on the station.  We will definitely be back and highly recommend a stay here for anyone planning a trip to the Flinders Ranges.

Mount Little Station – 10/10
If you’re after a genuine outback experience that’s equal parts rugged and soul restoring, Mount Little Station delivers in spades. Set in the jaw dropping Flinders Ranges, this place isn’t just a campsite, it’s a deep breath for the spirit.

The station strikes the perfect balance between wild and welcoming. Camp spots are thoughtfully spaced with epic views of ancient ranges, weathered trees, and golden light that makes even your dusty 4WD look photogenic. Facilities are clean, practical, and honest, just like the people who run the place. There’s even a rustic honesty box store tucked into a hand-built stone shed, stocked with local crafts and charm.

You can lose hours wandering the ruins and relics scattered across the property. Every old fence post, stove, or crumbling wall tells a story. It’s like stepping into a living museum, minus the velvet ropes and gift shop. Whether you’re stargazing from your swag, hiking up Mount Little itself, or just sitting by the fire letting the silence do its thing, this is the kind of place that reminds you why you travel.

The team here have done something rare, they’ve kept it simple, real, and full of heart. There’s no flash, just raw beauty, deep time, and space to reconnect with what matters.
Great camping spot, friendly people.

We really enjoyed exploring this amazing property so many beautiful spots as well as interesting historical sites.We had a huge area for our caravan which felt like we were the only campers there. The owners also have an amazing Woodford pizza night at there outback style tavern under the stars with a fireplace to sit around and chat to other travellers  on Wednesday and saturday nights.The best pizzas and a host with friendly humour .

Five star, five star, five star! What a lovely experience, having facilities and events /activities that cater for evenyone, big and small. Stunning ladscape, delicious pizza, lovely campsites, best bush walks. Recommend to everyone! Will definitely be going back.
Such a fantastic, family friendly place to stay. Very informative & helpful with what’s to do on and around the station. Great hosts & so much fun! Thanks for having us!

Had the best time here this past weekend at the homestead with my family!!! The pizzas were amazing, and the driving trails were so much fun!! It was also great having all the animals around, especially the donkeys!!

Came here to sing. The lamb pizza was amongst the best ld ever had. Thank you for catering to my needs with some of the toppings. What an unforgettable experience i had here.  I absolutely loved it. From getting to hold a baby joey in a pouch to meeting camels, donkeys, wild goats, kangaroos, different fowls and lambs at the homestead too. Saw emus on my way in and out. There's cows and sheep and a sheep took a liking to me and followed me round in the morning and I could touch and cuddle it. I've never touched a sheep before or a joey and all the animals l petted.  The kangaroos have red fur on their backs here and are not just all grey like lm used to. Thankyou to the lovely lady who gave me food to hand feed the animals and having a chat with me. If lm ever over that way again l would definitely come stay a couple nights.  I hope our festival group can come back next year to perform again. The land is so different to in Victoria. I was stunned and in awe of my whole experience at Mt little and the size of the property is huge. I'd highly recommend Mt Little to anyone.

lovely homestead with plenty of rooms and has great views. The owners are very accomodating take time out of their day to help with any issues! It was nice to see wildlife and the farm animals on the property particularly the donkeys.
Such a fantastic location, can’t really fault anything here- beautiful views, fun pizza night amenities were excellent and animals made it  lots of fun, we will return thanks Kellie .

Definitely need a All wheel Drive for the Road throughout this property.
Fun Tressure Hunt around the property, so much fun .We stayed at a Off Grid Camp - site.
PIZZA night is brilliant with all kinds of toppings Byo or buy at the register when ordering your pizza .
Great atmosphere.
WOW these stars sre incredible.
They have showers on the property best to use when the sun is on the Solar panels. And toilets.
We have Starlink & Cowfish
Big help with services.
Totally recommend this stay , we had 4 nights here .
We also did a Day trip to Rawnsley airstrip did a flight over The Flinders Rangers. Unbelievable. Must do .
Mt Little STATION  has Lost of animals to feed .Brilliant for young kids or grown up ones.
We stay here again we came the 29th April to 3rd May
Weather so Gorgeous
9° nights  22 -24° Days Blue skies Clear nights perfect time of the year to stay.
Kellie & Peter the owners are extremely friendly & Kind .Happy to help in any way.

Great place to stop and explore parts of the Flinders Rangers. Extra large camp sites. No close neighbours. Bring your friends (we could have fit 10 vans in our area with room to spare.) If you really want you don't have to leave the property to explore totally amazing. Welcome pack very informative with so many options of things to do. Pizza Nights were yummy to say the least. We were lucky to experience 3 of these during our stay! With a record breaking over 230 pizzas in one night cooked. Our hosts Kellie and Peter love a beer and chat. The most easy going and accommodating people you could ask for. So much to see and do. We stayed a week.

Just home after our 9th visit and as always we were incredibly sad to pull out. One of our family’s favourite spots, all made possible by the unwavering dedication of Kellie and Peter and the team in making Mt Little something unique with so many ways to fill each day.
It wouldn’t be a visit without taking part in a pizza night at the tavern - amazing food and atmosphere every time.

Amazing station experience! So much to see and do around the station and area. Will be back!
Also do not miss the pizza night, mt little lamb special best pizza I’ve ever had!

Just spent 6 nights with 5 other families on a secluded camp site with awesome Mountain View’s. We spent days exploring the property feeding the animals and just relaxing in the peaceful surroundings. Our hosts Kellie & Peter made us feel right at home. Pizza night was a hit with everyone. I highly recommend Mt little station to anyone
Absolutely amazing stay! The property is huge, heaps to do, caravan sites are enormous! Get yourself there and take it all in!

What an amazing Easter we had, Peter and Kellie and your staff opened your property with open arms. We saw the best of the flinders, the pizza night was to die for especially the lamb pizza....Yummmmm! Surrounding people who stayed on the station were friendly and kept the amenities to pristine condition.
The cave was a hard walk, but perfection when we hit the top. Roy's store was adorable with great souvenirs to take home and remember our experience! The 4wd track to the top of Mt Little was well worth the drive with bragging rights to put our names on a rock and add it to the pile. We absolutely loved every minute of this stay.

Third time camping at Mt Little Station and again had a brilliant time. Fully booked and still felt so secluded on our campsite. So much to see and do and the pizzas again were amazing. Will definitely be back!

Just beautiful! We stayed 2 nights here. So fun exploring the station and seeing those beautiful sunrises and sunsets. The pizza night is one we will remember forever. The kids loved the private walk and feeding the baby roo. Thank you for having us. Ps we love the merch too.

Just returned from Mt Little Station spending Easter camping. With kids ranging from 2yo to 6yo, this was the perfect spot to take them camping. 4x4 driving, pirate cave, music, pizzas, shearing, petting zoo plus the usual camping activities/site seeing. Surprisingly relaxing for the parents and kids always entertained. Well done to the Mt Little team on what was a very busy weekend.

What an amazing Easter,  Peter and kellie you and your staff should be extremely proud. We can't thank you enough for welcoming us onto your property and allowing us to explore. We had a blast. Something for everyone,  kids loved the animals and art's n crafts, we all loved exploring the the tracks, pizza night was fabulous and very yummy, well done 🥰

Great weekend here for Easter. Lots of people on the property but you wouldn’t have known it as there is plenty of room to explore. Pizzas were top notch and came out quickly even though they were catering to many. Camp sites spread out well and was a bonus to have flushing toilets at a station stay! Thanks for sharing your property with us.

We are a young family of 5 and we stayed here for 3 nights.
They’ve really thought of everything here.
From baby animal feeding mornings, walks with pirate treasure at the end (the coins are a great touch!), donkey feeding, a swimming hole and a pizza night that brought the whole family happiness.
Great facilities, great hosts, amazing views.
Do yourself a favour and stay st Mt Little Station if you’re in the area! We will certainly be back!

Brilliant place to stay for a day ,week or month. Excellent friendly hosts and plenty to see and do. The tavern and pizzas are just the cherry on top. Can't rate this place highly enough. If you're heading to the Flinders Ranges and don't stay here for a night or two you should give yourself an uppercut.

Great hosts with great amenities.  The tavern is top notch and the pizzas are that good they'd give a jelly fish a horn . Book yourself a spot now. You will not be disappointed plenty to see and do for all ages .  And you'll get a mountain of information in your welcome pack to keep you informed.
Beautiful location, the views are fantastic, great 4-wheel drive tracks, loved the hiking trails. The hosts are very friendly and helpful. Highly recommend!!
Beautiful spot! Pizza night was a hit! Owners were friendly and very helpful. Would definitely stay here again

Got pretty fun scene there  : solar showers , dirty dongas with death flies on the pillows and on the bed ( plus some on the floor ) and a “ gravel road “ that nearly smashed the car . The way is alternating from gravel to very rocky road ( must recommend 4WD ). NO data reception . We understand is an outback area , but the prices are overcharging . Waste of fuel

The only good thing is hips of wild life crossing the way .

What a great part of Australia. An amazing landscape with amazing people. Thanks to Kellie and her family for looking after us at Mount Little Station. We loved it and highly recommend staying over if you get the chance.

Just had another amazing stay at Mt Little Station. Peter and Kellie make you feel very welcome and are happy to allow you to explore and enjoy their awesome property. They are very accommodating even when incredibly busy mustering and shearing the sheep. There is so much to do and see, both on the station and at the stunning Flinders Ranges. I’ll always cherish my times there, thank you Peter and Kellie.
Station Maps are not accurate gorgeous camp sites good camp showers only bad thing is poor maps and too may barb wire fences over the track I happened 2 miss 1 and now gotta pack my tyres

Two nights is definitely not enough here.
We spent 2 nights in the dongas, much to our teenage son’s initial horror of having no power 😂.
After a few days of disconnecting he admitted it was the highlight of our 2 week outback trip.
Comfy beds, starry nights sitting on the deck chatting and laughing, the camels, donkeys, Roos, emus, ruins, 4wd tracks and pizza night. Yes the camp kitchen and bathrooms are rustic but totally functional. That’s was it’s all about. We will definately be back, and like the flies, we will bring more people! (And a stronger bash plate for our 4wd!) 🤦🏽‍♀️

We came here and spent 4 wonderful nights in a bush camp site it was just peaceful and relaxing at night around the campfire.We drove around and explored the station and saw lots of animals.
The pizza night was lovely, and to add in the table service by Peter.
Beautiful spot, pizza night was great and met some lovely people ❤️

Wow just wow! This place is amazing it has something for everyone. A photographer’s paradise from wildlife to landscape to old ruins. A 4x4 enthusiast playground many tracks to take . Heaps for kids to do even a natural spring to swim in. Sad we are leaving today could spend a lot more time here and use as a base to explore the rest of the flinders. Sites are spacious and private I could go on and on…so yourself a favour and experience what this place has to offer

After reading other reviews and having stayed, I am unsure where others have actually stayed. Yes the campsite was very large. Yes our neighbours were not very close, nor was the amenities block, which was not very clean. As for the "supposedly" camp kitchen, I will let the photos speak for themselves.  After 18 months of experiencing different campgrounds, this has set a new level for creative advertising. Perhaps the owners should spend a couple of nights up the road at Rawnsley Park and see how it can be done well. If you are going to charge these prices for fully self supported unpowered sites, then provide better facilities.

Stayed here on the recommendation of fellow travellers. The camp site was fantastic, amazing views. Loads of things to do, from 4WD tracks to hiking,  treasure hunts and animal feeding for kids. Pizza night was an added bonus. I would recommend anyone to stay here, the station hosts were very welcoming.

This place is magical. There’s something about the landscape - so vast, silent. The station is incredibly well appointed and laid out. Loads of campsites. We stayed on number 60, Mt Little Lookout. It was great, very open, the night time skies were insane. The hosts of this station are very friendly and helpful. We fed the baby animals in the morning. Watched the sunsets in the evening. Plenty to do during the day. Pizza night was also just magic. Highly recommend visiting.

We stopped here for the Pizza night on a Wednesday on the way home from Lake Eyre. We had our own vehicles and campers so just stayed in an unpowered site. The spot was great, flushing toilet facility a short walk from our spot and there seemed to be many scattered around. Pizza night was also great, pretty pricey but no more than expected. The Mt Little beer is also a tasty drop. They have 4wd tracks and walks which we didn't get a chance to experience, we will stay here longer next time we're in the area.

Great spot!
There is heaps of room on sites, with plenty of space for the kids to explore. Home stead had bags of wood to purchase so we were able to have a camp fire.
We were there for pizza night and the pizza was great.
The atmosphere on pizza night was great too with all sites coming together for food and a drink.

Old amenities and cold shower at 2.30pm on a sunny day: if the solar system is not sufficient for the number of people staying, then it should be upgraded to gas.
Apart from that the campsite we were allocated was pleasant with nice views.

We had a beautiful bush camp site away from other campers. Sunsets were spectacular. Loved driving around the property looking at the many historical sites. Thoroughly enjoyed the Black Goat walk. We were fortunate to be visiting the Mt. Little Station on one of their pizza nights. Delicious pizzas and joined a table where we enjoyed the company of some other campers.

We spent 3 nights at Mt Little Station Flinders Ranges and had a fantastic stay.
Camp-site areas are big, so if you are travelling in a group you would fit on one site.
Amenities are solar powered so shower when the sun's up, the water is hot and the water pressure is amazing.
There is so much to see and do at Mt Little Station, the 4wd track has great views from the top of the hill. You could spend a couple of days doing the walking tracks and drives on the property.
The animals are all very friendly and come up for a pat.
The tavern has delicious pizzas on set nights, the atmosphere around the fire there is really nice. The owners come out and have a drink and chat with you and are very welcoming.
This place suits everyone young and old and is pet friendly.
A 20 minute drive from Hawker if you need some groceries and Telstra service is available through most parts of the station.
I highly recommend staying there.

Amazing sites with stunning views, you feel as though you're the only one there apart from the Emus, kangaroos, goats and sheep until pizza night at the Tavern - great feed. Our hosts Kellie and Peter are brilliant and very welcoming.

Kellie was Awesome, fantastic experience our group loved everything about Mt Little Station. Great 4WD Tracks not to hard, beautiful walks and the Tavern went off with the Wood Fired Pizzas....................We will be back.
PS. Don't forget to purchase a BBQ Pack🥂🍻🍷

Great location, wonderful campsites and walks.
Pizza night a highlight.

We stayed at this amazing spot on the June long weekend. Our hosts were like old friends , we were made feel welcome and the relaxed pace and authenticity of the station lifestyle was amazing. Views, serenity, sunsets and pizzas. We will be back and show more friends this beautiful place

Third time back to Mount Little and it keeps getting better, Pete and Kellie have done an amazing job creating one of, if not THE best Station Stay in South Australia. The Tavern experience Saturday night was a highlight again; live music, tasty pizza, watching the sunset light up Elders Range. Looking forward to next time!

We stayed at Mt. little last weekend and throughly enjoyed our stay, from animal feeds, craft classes, massages, pizzas, 4wd tracks & peaceful walks along a water gorge there is something here for everyone.
I have highly recommended this station to everyone I know!
Absolutely amazing scenery in every direction 😍 will 100% be returning with more friends
An excellent weekend away is guaranteed at Mt Little Station. The pizza night a real treat.

Fantastic place to stay, create memories.. enjoy wood oven pizza and watch the outstanding sunset. Connecting with nature is so good for the soul.
🐪🐐🐑🌅🌄🌇

Had a great time at Mt Little Station. Kids loved spending time with the animals and we loved the secluded camp spot with a spot for the fire. Walking out to Maya Gorge & 4WDriving up to the lookout were also highlights.

Booked a bush camp for 3 nights ended up staying for 6! Such a good base to do day trip throughout the Flinders. A gem of a place. Great large roomy  sites  to cater for all sized  groups. The views are breath taking with plenty of wild life. The tavern is a great spot on Pizza night and the pizzas are yummo. The hosts Kelli and Peter are awesome. Highly recommend a stay here but make sure you book for no less than 3 night as there is so much to do on the property, drives and walks. Great spot for kids to explore . Photos don’t do it justice 😁 Glenys and Craig

What a fantastic place to camp up for a few days. Kellie was very welcoming and informative when checking in, and Peter brought a lot of laughs at Pizza Night at the Tavern. Camp sites are well spaced to give each group some privacy, and 4WD tracks clearly marked to take you to some interesting spots on the property. Split redgum in bags is available to purchase for your evening campfires too. We’ll be back!

My partner and I had the pleasure of staying in the Range Retreat. We loved everything about it. The location is amazing. We relaxed in the outdoor baths under the stars with the fire going. The view is spectacular, especially the sunsets. We loved being able to hike up into different parts of the range, everywhere you turn it's beautiful. I love this part of South Australia.
Kellie is a great host. It was very easy to book and she was flexible when I had to change my booking dates.
We really hope to get back to Mt Little Station soon as there's so much more to see and experience.

Great Offgrid camping, beautiful location with private spots to camp. Think we’ve stayed here 3-4 times now,   Pizza night is epic, service and friendliness second to none.  Highly recommend staying here!

A great campsite, friendly hosts and plenty to do if you so desire. Nice walks, ruins to explore and drives around the property. Our second time there, and we will be back again for sure!! Thanks Kellie and Pete!

I can’t say enough good things about Mt Little station.  The scenery is spectacular and the camping excellent.  The owners do a fantastic job of running the place and are great hosts. Top this off with awesome sunsets and delicious wood oven pizzas 10/10

There aren't enough good words to describe the experience at Mt Little Station. The land itself is just gorgeous and there's so much to explore! Our kids loved the animal feeding, with Vanessa being extremely friendly and knowledgeable. The sites were the biggest we've ever stayed on, and the amenities were clean and provided a nice hot shower, with great pressure. A perfect spot to base yourself for exploration of the Ikara-Flinders Ranges NP. Oh and pizza night was pretty great too. We'd absolutely come back in a heartbeat!
Great place to stay in Flinders Ranges - plenty of room for vans and campers, interesting drives and great views,  pizza night memorable - great team.
Great hosts and clear starry nights :))
Awesome place with great campsites. Love the pizzas and animal feeds

Friendly station owners. Hot showers and flushing toilets.
We were on a bush campsite plenty of room and far enough from the other site.
No extra charge to do the 4wd tracks if you wanted to explore on your own.  there's a little easy walk you could do also. Family friendly highly recommend.  There were animals our kids could pat and feed when we stayed there during Easter. We won't forget their pizza night either

After 3 years travelling Australia this is still one of our most favourite spots.
A true outback experience where you are welcomed as one of the family.
We always make the stop when in SA.
The pizza nights up at the tavern are awesome and all the extra activities to do on the property. You can stay for days and have so much to do you don’t need to leave but if you do decide to head out it’s the perfect spot to explore the flinders ranges.
We can’t wait to go back for our 5 star camping and adventure experience.
Our son always asks when are we going back to Mt Little station.

Stunning place. Had the whole family over, some from interstate, for a memorial weekend for our parents and brother. Couldn't have been more perfect. The homestead and the sunset apartments were fabulous, had everything we needed. The property is stunning. Plenty of places to hike and 4wd. Felt like we were the only ones there, even though there were other campers on the property, it's just so big! Kellie was a delight to liaise with. And pizza night...what a treat! Will definitely we back.
Mount Little is seriously one of the best places on earth.  Great location, great people and the pizzas are amazing!  Thank you for having us back every year!

Stayed in our caravan last year in September and again this year in April because we loved it so much. Communication with Kellie from the booking process, information packs and things to do was easy and straightforward. We loved our campsites, well away from other campers and there was something to keep everyone happy, from glorious sunsets, campfires, the animals, walks and 4WD tracks full of local history. Our favourite walks were the Jack and Jill Hill and the River of Rocks! We also enjoyed an evening at the tavern, soaking up the atmosphere along with seriously delicious pizzas served by friendly staff. Bonus was finishing up with a hot shower at the end of dusty days!
Been coming here for 4 years and love every bit of it ! Best pizzas in SA !

Great place. Relaxed owners that let you do your own thing. The whole property is open for you to explore. They even encourage you to explore and find new things because as they are still learning themselves. Go 4wding with out paying extra dollars for the privilege. Oh yeah and the walk up the Mayo Gorge every morning. Beautiful.

My Sister and I have been going to Mt Little since they put the big sign up at the front gate. We have watched it grow over the years and is now a wonderful family holiday destination. Camping , caravan , cabin or the beautifully renovated cottage and there is always pizza night or star gazing or just wandering the the property keep your eye out for the wallabies eagles kangaroos and the birds are something else Mt Little has something for everyone-GO THERE-your hosts Pete and Kellie are the best 👏👏👏‼️🌻

Kids absolutely loved every minute! Great base to base yourself in the ranges, loved pizza night and kids activities. The information pack is so well thought out and organised all the thinking is done for you. Loved it!

One of the most amazing farm stays we have ever been. From the second you drive in you are greeted by the owners at the house. From there you make your way to your campsite and its only then do you truely get to see the marvel and unbelievable size of this place. So many walks and drives to do. We drove in a convoy with other campers to the top of Mount Little and it was absolutely spectacular.
Highly recommend this place and we are hoping to get back there at some stage this year
A great spot to camp. The hosts were sensational and well informed.  The pizza nights are a must. Great views, and the facilities were well maintained.  Loved our stay there. Will return.
Magnificent outback experience on a working station. Hosts go above and beyond to accommodate visitors, and plenty of station tracks to get around and visit the sights.
An incredible peace of paradise! Peter & Kellie leave no detail behind. An unforgettable experience marked in everyones memories forever!
Amazing station stay. So much to do and explore. Campsites are spacious and private and love the pizzas at the Tavern!

Peter and Kellie and crew are doing a great thing here. Really accommodating and warm hosts, great rates, an immersive and authentic Australiana experience. The kids loved the animal feeding. Be sure to try and line up your stay with the pizza nights (Wednesday and Saturday) as this is a great thing to do here too.
Kellie and Peter are awesome hosts. We enjoyed our stay immensely. Scenery, wildlife, farm animals, 4WD tracks, pizzas - fantastic. Do yourself a favour - best station stay.

Nestled in the heart of the Flinders Ranges, Mount Little Station and camping ground offer a secluded haven for nature enthusiasts. Priced at $15 per person per night, it provides a wallet-friendly escape into the vast beauty of the region. $20 Fee for firewood.

The camping ground itself is surprisingly spacious, offering ample room to set up camp without feeling crowded. While you can spot neighbors in the distance, the generous space ensures a sense of privacy. An added bonus is the proximity to a pizza night, just a 5-minute drive up the road for $25 pizzas – a tempting option if you're not in the mood for campfire cooking. Although it's uncertain if the pizza night is a nightly occurrence, it adds a convenient dining alternative.

Beyond the camping ground, Mount Little Station is strategically located close to walking tracks and lookouts, making it an ideal base for exploration. The staff's friendliness further enhances the overall experience, providing a welcoming atmosphere.

One of the highlights of the stay is the mesmerizing night sky. While you may not have captured many pictures of the location, the countless snapshots of the celestial display will undoubtedly become cherished memories. If I find myself back in the Flinders Ranges, Mount Little Station would undoubtedly be my choice, offering both a comfortable camping experience and the opportunity to immerse myself in the natural wonders of the region.
These people did'nt bother to return our call requesting a booking!!

Amazing station to stay on.
Accommodation available onsite or camping with no power or water available.
Located just out of Hawker lots of driving and sites to see onsite.
Toilers and showers are extremely clean and well maintained.
Camp kitchen limited but has everything needed.

What an amazing property and the staff and owners are very helpful and friendly. We had 5 days off grid in a quiet location just relaxing. Lots of great tracks/drives in 4WD and some difficult climbs. Beautiful views all around, Sunset lookout, Yellow Valley, Mayo Gorge walking, hiking etc etc. Some original owner ruins that are being restored by dedicated staff. The Tavern is great with wicked pizzas on Wednesday and Saturday, my wife is a very sensitive Coeliac and had no problems.
We will definitely be returning. Thank you Peter and Kellie for your hospitality.
Regards Michael and Beck

Camping holiday with van, stunning scenery, sunset in the Elder Range was a joy each night. Private campsites and good facilities. lots in interesting walks and drives and one of the best info packs we have seen.  Great job Peter and Kellie. So close to Hawker it’s an easy drive from down south

Thanks Kellie+ Peter for welcoming Harry+ me to your amazing station+ so thoughtfully allocating us a flat site for our tent. Wish we could have stayed longer+ done a tour but hope we can come back for longer next winter. Loved the emus especially+ the peace+ tranquillity.

We stayed in the dongas. Beds were super comfortable. We were a bit worried about being cold as the rooms have no electricity but it wasn't a problem at all. They were perfectly fine. Showers were hot and clean. Pizza night at the Tavern was amazing.
